ObjectVnums.brief: A collection of constants representing the unique virtual number identifiers (vnumbers) for various game objects.
ObjectVnums.details: The ObjectVnums group defines a comprehensive set of constexpr integer constants, each representing a specific game object identified by a unique vnum. These objects include character corpses (both NPC and player), severed body parts, weapons, light sources, portals, maps, quest items, and various prototypes used throughout the game. By centralizing these vnum definitions, the group ensures consistent and maintainable referencing of objects across different modules, facilitating object creation, recognition, and management. The organization reflects the diverse range of in-game items and prototypes, supporting both gameplay mechanics and system extensibility.
ObjectVnums.notes: Constants are organized to group related objects logically, such as corpses, weapons, and special prototypes. This organization aids in maintaining clarity and ease of updates when adding or modifying object vnums.
ObjectVnums.rationale: The purpose of the ObjectVnums group is to provide a standardized, centralized set of identifiers for game objects, reducing errors from hardcoded values and simplifying object handling across the codebase. This approach enhances code readability, maintainability, and consistency in object management, supporting the overall system architecture by ensuring reliable references to game items and prototypes.
ObjectVnums.name: ObjectVnums

OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC.brief: The virtual number identifier for NPC corpse objects within the game.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC.details: OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC is a constant set to 10, used to uniquely identify corpse objects generated from NPC characters. It ensures that all NPC corpses are consistently referenced and managed throughout the game systems, such as looting, decay, and resurrection mechanics. This constant is passed to object creation functions to instantiate a corpse with the correct virtual number, maintaining uniformity in object handling and identification.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC.notes: Using a dedicated constant for NPC corpse virtual numbers helps prevent magic numbers in the code and simplifies updates if the identifier needs to change.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC.rationale: This constant exists to standardize the identification of NPC corpses, enabling consistent handling and processing of these objects across various game functionalities.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC.name: OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_NPC

usages.fight_8cc, void make_corpse(Character *c: Prototype used to create a corpse object for NPC characters.

OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC.brief: The constant representing the virtual number (vnum) for player character corpse objects.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC.details: OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC is a predefined constant that holds the vnum used to identify player character corpse objects within the game environment. Initialized with the value 11, it is utilized whenever the system needs to create, reference, or manipulate a corpse object corresponding to a player character. This constant promotes consistency and clarity across the codebase, particularly in functions responsible for generating or handling player corpses, such as in the make_corpse function. Its use ensures that all player corpse objects are uniformly identified by the same vnum, facilitating reliable object management and interactions.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time constexpr, ensuring its value remains immutable and optimized during compilation. It is specifically designated for player character corpses, distinguishing them from other object types within the game.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC.rationale: This constant exists to standardize the identification of player corpse objects, simplifying object creation and reference processes, and ensuring consistent behavior when handling player-related death events.
OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC.name: OBJ_VNUM_CORPSE_PC
